This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 2:

OSC have an page aligned transfer ONLY. exception of first page for non aligned DIRECT IO, but again it may covered on OSC/OST level - without any changes on LNet.

Patch Set 2:

IMO, LNet is not a module that should only serve OSC forever.

Patch Set 2:

ok. MDC<>MDT have a read dir page transfer, page aligend.
OSC <> OST have a buffered IO, Direct IO. buffered IO always page aligend.
Direct IO "should" page aligned per POSIX description, but per LLNL request lustre to support unaligned DIO to avoid first page read before write.
That mode submit first page with some offset, other pages in same range - start is page aligned, end may not aligned for last page.

Patch Set 2:

as I explained on the sge patch, non-aligned first page will shift all following pages on router because router buffer always use 0 as offset, and double fragments.

Patch Set 2:

What is this unaligned DIO change for LLNL that you refer to?

parents.subject: LU-8413 llite: specify READA debug mask for sanity_101f

commit.subject: LU-5718 lnet: add offset for selftest brw
commit.message:
LU-5718 lnet: add offset for selftest brw

In current lnet selftest, both client and server side bulk have
no offset and we can only test page aligned IO, this patch changed
this:

- user can set brw offset by lst add_test ... brw off=OFFSET ...
- offset is only effective on client side so far
- to simply implementation, offset needs to be eight bytes aligned

Signed-off-by: Liang Zhen <liang.zhen@intel.com>
Change-Id: I34690d2c8fbb627b827291afcd46d7aa59831a3f
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/12496
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Doug Oucharek <doug.s.oucharek@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
